title: echinochloa crus-galli var. crus-pavonis
intro: Echinochloa crus-galli var. crus-pavonis, also known as barnyard grass or barnyard millet, is a type of annual grass native to Asia and Australia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. Echinochloa crus-galli var. crus-pavonis is believed to offer several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using it in combination with other herbs in a decoction or tincture.
